summary: telebot-bot-run installs a Telegram-driven remote shell and Pastebin dropper on import
details: On `import telebot_bot_run`, the package performs two attacker-controlled operations against the installer. First, top-level code fetches https://pastebin.com/raw/FTLjhBMX with `requests.get` and passes the response body directly to `exec()`, giving the author arbitrary code execution on the installer host with mutable, anonymously-hosted content. Second, the module unconditionally calls `start_bot()`, which spawns a background thread connecting a hardcoded Telegram bot token (8951969280:...) with owner ID 7959603290 to the Telegram Bot API. Registered handlers include `/ssh <cmd>` routed to `subprocess.run(command, shell=True)`, `/get <path>` that opens arbitrary files and returns them via `bot.send_document`, `/collect <dir>` that zips a directory with `shutil.make_archive` and uploads it, plus `/del`, `/mkdir`, `/make`, and document-upload handlers. The Telegram channel gives any party controlling the bot full remote shell access and arbitrary file read/write on every host that imports the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
